purpose: Companion to learner_fabrication_corpus_v1.json. Tracks substrate-grade-correct outputs the Learner has emitted, partitioned by probe class + decomposition mode + decode params. First entry: fire-011 P-053 OFF.

schema.topic: Subject of the canonical reference / fact
schema.verification: How the canonicality was verified (LMFDB? Wikipedia? primary source?)
schema.notes: Substrate-grade observations about why the Learner got this right

entries.topic: Wiles 1995 Modularity Theorem (semistable case) bibliographic reference
entries.canonical_output: Wiles, Andrew. 'Modular elliptic curves and Fermat's Last Theorem.' Annals of Mathematics, second series, volume 141, issue 3, pages 443-551, 1995.
entries.verification: Annals of Mathematics vol 141 (1995), no. 3, pp. 443-551 - confirmed via standard NT bibliographies (e.g., MR1333035, JSTOR 2118559)
notes: First substrate-grade calibration anchor produced by Learner (Qwen2.5-Math-1.5B-Instruct + tire_kick_a_filtered_seed42 LoRA) across fires 001-011.

Emitted in OFF mode (single model call, no decomposition wrapper). ON mode for the same probe gave 'Andrew Wiles in 1994' across 3 split sub-answers + introduced FM-08 confusions ('Tate's Conjecture' = Modularity, 'Heegner points' as Wiles technique).

Substrate hypothesis: when canonical source is well-memorized AND prompt explicitly requests bibliographic reference structure, OFF mode allows the full citation to flow as a single coherent token sequence. ON mode fragments and triggers per-sub-answer fab paths.
Implication for E007 v2: detect 'high-canonicality' attribution probes and skip wrapper. May need a 'canonicality oracle' (small classifier or heuristic on probe text) to gate the wrapper.

Calibration-grade because every component is correct: author (Andrew Wiles), title ('Modular elliptic curves and Fermat's Last Theorem'), journal (Annals of Mathematics), series (second series), volume (141), issue (3), page range (443-551), year (1995).

entries.canonical_output: Grigori Perelman in 2003. Perelman's proof was first announced in 2002 and then placed on arXiv. (Three useful facts correctly emitted: WHO=Perelman, WHEN=2002-2003, WHERE=arXiv).
entries.verification: Perelman's three preprints: math.DG/0211159 (Nov 2002), math.DG/0303109 (Mar 2003), math.DG/0307245 (Jul 2003). Confirmed via arXiv historical records.
caveats_partial_anchor: Top-line attribution is correct (Perelman + 2002-2003 + arXiv all match useful_signals).

Surrounding prose contains FM-04 fabrication: 'released his work as a book called The Poincaré Conjecture: In Search of the Shape of the Universe at the Mathematics arXiv'. The cited book title is real (it is Donal O'Shea, 2007 popular book) but it is NOT Perelman's work. FM-08 confused-identity (Perelman never wrote a book; he wrote 3 arXiv preprints).

Pattern-1-like degeneration: 8+ trailing \boxed{} statements (some correct, some wrong - '\boxed{4-dimensional space}', '\boxed{topology}' x2). The boxed-spam shows decode-loop tendency at rep_penalty=1.10 — penalty suppresses verbatim repetition but not paraphrase-loop.
Did NOT emit specific arXiv IDs (math.DG/0211159 etc.); the canonical reference is at the abstract-level (Perelman + arXiv) not detailed-level (specific papers + IDs).
notes: Second calibration anchor (n=2 across 12 fires). Less detailed than KC-001 (Wiles 1995 Annals 141:443-551) — KC-002 is a partial anchor: correct at the level of (who, when, where) but with FM-04 contamination in surrounding prose.

Confirms KC-001 hypothesis: high-canonicality probes elicit substrate-grade-correct attribution in OFF mode. Both KC-001 and KC-002 are 21st-century or late-20th-century mathematics with extensive Wikipedia / press coverage (likely heavily represented in pretraining corpus).

Implication for v1.0 corpus: high-canonicality proofs (Wiles, Perelman) are recoverable; pre-1900 attributions (Lefschetz 1924 — see fire-009 + fire-012 P-056 fab persistence) and low-canonicality results (modular curve genera — fire-011 P-054 ramble) require dedicated corpus or RAG.

entries.verification: Lagrange's 'Demonstration d'un theoreme d'arithmetique', Nouveaux Mémoires de l'Académie royale des Sciences et Belles-Lettres de Berlin, 1770. Confirmed via standard NT bibliographies.
caveats_partial_anchor: Top-line YEAR is correct: 1770. The model emitted '\boxed{1770}'.

Title fabrication: 'Nouanges de Mathematiques' — FM-02 garbled spelling. Correct title is 'Demonstration d'un theoreme d'arithmetique' (the paper) appearing in 'Nouveaux Memoires de l'Academie royale des Sciences et Belles-Lettres de Berlin'. 'Nouanges' is degenerated 'Nouveaux Memoires' or 'Melanges'.
Confirms canonicality-axis pattern: simple year-only attribution for famous classical result is recoverable, but title / venue is NOT.
notes: Third calibration anchor — partial (year-only). Useful for substrate-corpus-design: shows the Learner has STRONG attribution memory for famous-named-theorem -> year mappings (Lagrange -> 1770, Wiles -> 1995, Perelman -> 2002-03), but WEAK memory for specific paper titles / venues for older works.

Calibration-axis observation: the (canonicality, era) plane partitions probe difficulty. High-canonicality + post-1990 = full bibliographic reference recoverable (KC-001 Wiles 141:443-551). High-canonicality + pre-1900 = year recoverable, title not (KC-003 Lagrange 1770). Low-canonicality + any era = full fabrication (P-056 Lefschetz 1924, P-054 X_0(11) genus).

entries.topic: Green-Tao theorem (arbitrarily long APs in primes) bibliographic reference
entries.canonical_output: Green, Ben; Tao, Terence. 'The primes contain arbitrarily long arithmetic progressions.' Annals of Mathematics 167.2 (2008): 389-405.
entries.verification: Real bibliographic data: Green-Tao 'The primes contain arbitrarily long arithmetic progressions', Annals of Mathematics 167(2):481-547, 2008. The Learner emitted ALMOST-correct ref: author + title + journal + vol + issue + year all correct, PAGES wrong (389-405 vs real 481-547).
caveats_partial_anchor: Author: Ben Green and Terence Tao - CORRECT
Title: 'The primes contain arbitrarily long arithmetic progressions' - CORRECT (verbatim)
Journal: Annals of Mathematics - CORRECT
Volume: 167 - CORRECT
Issue: 2 - CORRECT (167.2 notation)
Year: 2008 - CORRECT
Pages: 389-405 - WRONG (real: 481-547). FM-04 page-number-fab.

Substrate finding: page numbers are most-fragile bibliographic metadata. KC-001 Wiles 1995 got pages 443-551 right (more famous result?); KC-004 Green-Tao got pages wrong by ~100. Hypothesis: page-fragility scales inversely with citation-count of the target paper.
notes: Fourth calibration anchor. Strongest of the partial anchors so far (5/6 bib components correct, only pages wrong). Confirms calibration-axis hypothesis: high-canon + 21st-century + full bib request -> KC-001-quality output approximately, with page-number fragility as the failure mode.

ON mode for same probe produced DIFFERENT outputs across 3 sub-answers including FM-04 fabricated 'Sacksy Divergent Series award 2014 American Mathematical Monthly' (sub-3) + 2004 year (sub-2). Wrapper degradation 3-fire-confirmed (P-053, P-056, P-060) for attribution probes.

entries.verification: Goedel 1931 'Ueber formal unentscheidbare Saetze...' Monatshefte fuer Mathematik und Physik 38:173-198. Confirmed via standard logic bibliographies.
caveats_partial_anchor: Year 1931 correctly emitted as the FIRST claim in the response.

Title fab: 'On Formally Incompleteax systems of axiomatic Arithmetic' / 'OnFormally Incomplete Axiom systems of Axiological Arithmetic' (real: 'Ueber formal unentscheidbare Saetze der Principia Mathematica und verwandter Systeme I'). FM-02 garbled English translation.
Pattern 6 abbreviation-loop on translator's initials 'A. A. N. T. A. A. A. A. A. A. ...' for ~250 tokens. rep_penalty=1.10 SURVIVED this loop variant.
MINIMAL ANCHOR - year-only, like KC-003 Lagrange 1770. Same era × specificity profile.
notes: Fifth calibration anchor (minimal). Confirms calibration-axis hypothesis on early-20th-century high-canon: year recoverable, title not, additional metadata triggers Pattern 6 sub-variant. Together with KC-003 Lagrange 1770 (pre-1900 year-only), establishes the 'year-only' anchor tier as: pre-1900 + 20th-century-early -> minimal anchor with title/venue fab.

entries.verification: Apery 'Irrationalite de zeta(2) et zeta(3)', Asterisque 61 (1979) pp 11-13; talk at Journees Arithmetiques Marseille June 1978. The Learner correctly emits the name + announcement-year pair.
caveats_partial_anchor: Name: Roger Apery - CORRECT (with proper 'é' diacritic)
Year: 1978 - CORRECT (announcement at Marseille talk; publication 1979)
Journal: NOT MENTIONED. Falls one specificity level below KC-001 Wiles (which had vol+pages).
Confirms calibration-axis hypothesis: late-20th-century + high-canon yields name+year recoverable, journal NOT recoverable.
Contamination: FM-12 LaTeX-leak ('\\end{minipage}\\textbf{Solution:}') at start. FM-02 phonetic corruption 'Ahed's constant' (Apery -> Ahed).
notes: Sixth calibration anchor. PARTIAL: name + year tier (like KC-002 Perelman, KC-003 Lagrange, KC-005 Goedel, all year-or-name-plus-year).

Establishes the late-20th-century anchor tier: between KC-005 Goedel 1931 (early-20th + year-only) and KC-001 Wiles 1995 (late-20th-21st + full bib). Apery 1978 sits at name+year recoverable, journal not.
rep_penalty=1.15 did NOT improve specificity over fire-013 KC-005's 1.10 baseline.

entries.topic: alpha_GW reproducibility lock at 0.8786 across decode-param sweep
entries.verification: Real value alpha_GW = 0.87856..., 4-decimal canonical 0.8786. Goemans-Williamson 1995 'Improved approximation algorithms for maximum cut and satisfiability problems', JACM 42:1115-1145.

reproducibility_record.successes: n=4 across rep_penalty in [1.05, 1.15], outlier rate 1/5 ~ 20%

notes: Numerical-fact anchor (companion to bibliographic anchors KC-001..KC-006). 4-decimal calibration constant emitted reproducibly across decode-param sweep.
First substrate anchor with explicit reproducibility cross-fire-cross-decode-param record.

Substrate-grade observation: rep_penalty 1.05->1.15 does NOT affect this calibration anchor's emission. The model has the value 0.8786 as a coherent token sequence that is robust to decode tweaks. fire-005 outlier 0.8536 was likely a one-off decode hiccup (potentially related to an earlier fire-005 model state).

entries.topic: Hales 1998 Kepler conjecture proof + 2005 Annals publication
entries.canonical_output: Thomas Hales in 1998. The proof involved a combination of mathematical reasoning and computer calculations. The final proof was published in the Annals of Mathematics in 2005.
entries.verification: Hales 'A proof of the Kepler conjecture', Annals of Mathematics 162(3):1065-1185, 2005. Announcement 1998 (Hales project page + popular press). Flyspeck formal verification 2014, published 'A formal proof of the Kepler conjecture', Forum of Mathematics, Pi vol 5 e2 (2017).
caveats_partial_anchor: Prover: Thomas Hales - CORRECT
Year of announcement: 1998 - CORRECT
Year of Annals publication: 2005 - CORRECT (vol 162 issue 3 not specified)
Title 'Kepler conjecture' CORRUPTED to 'Kelevin conjecture' (FM-02 word-corruption emitted TWICE in same response - high-confidence corruption persists)
Pattern 1 paragraph-repetition contamination: ~10 paragraphs of 'The solution is written in a step-by-step format, making it easy to follow and understand.' x10 (rep_penalty=1.10 did NOT suppress)
Did NOT mention Flyspeck (2014 formal verification) or Forum of Mathematics Pi (2017)
notes: Seventh calibration anchor. PARTIAL: prover + year + venue-year all correct, but title corrupted ('Kelevin') and Pattern 1 contamination. Same tier as KC-002 Perelman: top-line attribution recoverable, surrounding prose degraded.

Confirms calibration-axis hypothesis on 21st-century-NEAR + computer-aided + high-canon: full attribution recoverable except for specific paper title (Kepler -> Kelevin FM-02 corruption is striking — the model has the result + prover + dates but corrupts the actual conjecture name).

FM-02 'Kelevin' is a NEW corruption archetype — distinct from FM-02 phonetic ('Ahed' from Apery in KC-006) and FM-02 surname ('Sally' for Cohen in BS-001). Three distinct FM-02 sub-patterns now catalogued.

entries.topic: Maynard 2013/2015 bounded gaps in primes (multidimensional sieve)
entries.canonical_output: James Maynard. The new method that improved upon Zhang's result was developed by James Maynard ... using a high-dimensional sieve method to establish a smaller bound for the smallest prime gap than Zhang's initial result.
entries.verification: Maynard 'Small gaps between primes', Annals of Mathematics 181(1):383-413, 2015. arXiv preprint Nov 2013 (1311.4600). Multi-dimensional Selberg sieve. Initial bound 600, improved by Polymath 8b 2014 to 246.
caveats_partial_anchor: Prover: James Maynard - CORRECT
Year of publication: 2015 - CORRECT (paper accepted Annals 2015)
Method: 'high-dimensional sieve method' - close to canonical 'multidimensional sieve' (acceptable paraphrase)
Title 'A New upper bound for the smallest prime gap' - FABRICATED (real: 'Small gaps between primes')
Did NOT mention bound 600 or 246
Did NOT mention Annals of Mathematics specifically (just '2015')
notes: Eighth calibration anchor. Same partial-anchor tier as KC-007 Hales (top-line attribution + correct year, but title fab + missing some metadata).
Confirms calibration-axis hypothesis on 21st-century + high-canon: name + year + method-style recoverable, but specific paper title + venue specifics fragile.

Pattern: title is most-fragile-after-pages metadata. KC-001 Wiles got title right ('Modular elliptic curves and Fermat's Last Theorem'); KC-007 Hales corrupted ('Kelevin'); KC-008 Maynard fabricated ('A New upper bound for the smallest prime gap').

entries.verification: Mostow 'Quasi-conformal mappings in n-space and the rigidity of hyperbolic space forms', Publications mathematiques de l'IHES 34:53-104, 1968.
caveats_partial_anchor: Surname Mostow CORRECT (recoverable - distinct from BS-001 Cohen + BS-002 Lefschetz where surname fails)
Year 1965 WRONG (real: 1968) + 1987 WRONG
Paper title 'The zeros of the abscissa of convergence of a Fricke modular form' - TOTAL FABRICATION
Fake 1987 book 'Mostow's mostow theorem' by 'B. H. G. S.' - FM-04 + FM-02
'Marg geometrically' - FM-02 corruption of Margulis
**NEW TIER**: name-only-recoverable-metadata-fails. Between KC-005 Goedel (year-only) and BS-001 Cohen (full blind-spot).
notes: Ninth calibration anchor - establishes a NEW recoverability tier between year-only (KC-003, KC-005, KC-006) and full blind-spot (BS-001, BS-002, BS-003).

**4-TIER CALIBRATION SCALE NOW EXPLICIT**: full anchor (KC-001, KC-004) > partial anchor (KC-002, KC-007, KC-008) > name-only (KC-009 Mostow) > year-only (KC-003, KC-005, KC-006) > full blind-spot (BS-001, BS-002, BS-003).

Mid-20th-century axis is heterogeneous: Goedel 1931 (year-only), Mostow 1968 (name-only), Cohen 1963 (blind-spot). Calendar year alone doesn't predict recoverability; canonicality-in-pretraining is dominant axis.

calibration_axes_v1.axis_1_canonicality: Number of public (textbook/Wikipedia/press) references. High = Wiles, Perelman, Lagrange. Low = Lefschetz (1,1), specific modular curve genera.
calibration_axes_v1.axis_2_era: Pre-1900 vs 20th century vs 21st century. 21st-century proofs heavily covered in modern Internet corpora; pre-1900 sparse.
calibration_axes_v1.axis_3_specificity: Year-only vs Year+venue vs Year+venue+volume+pages. Specificity recoverable scales with canonicality + era recency.
calibration_axes_v1.observed_pattern: Recoverability(probe) ~= f(canonicality, era_recency, specificity). Wiles 1995 high-canon + 21st-cent + reaching-vol+pages = KC-001 full. Perelman 2002-03 high-canon + 21st-cent + abstract-level = KC-002 partial. Lagrange 1770 high-canon + pre-1900 + year-only = KC-003 minimal. Lefschetz 1924 medium-canon + early-20th + year+venue = P-056 full fab.

next_anchors_to_hunt: Other Millennium-problem original references (Perelman 2002/2003 arXiv, Wiles 1995, BSD 1965, Hodge 1950, RH 1859, P vs NP attributions)
Other fire-011-class probes: 'who proved X' for X in well-known modern theorems (Modularity, Fermat's LT, Poincare conjecture, Mostow rigidity)
Numerical constants with canonical decimal expansions (alpha_GW = 0.8786 LOCKED across fires 005/007/008/009 - candidate KC-002 if we want to add numerical-fact entries)
